summary: Finalized the Home-Run Bat item source file `itharisen.c` enough to mark it matching. The PR added the item state table for Harisen, corrected one function call in dropped logic from `it_8026B390` to `Item_8026849C`, updated `configure.py` so `melee/it/items/itharisen.c` builds as `Matching`, and localized several previously global symbols in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` to compiler-style local labels.
what_changed: Added `ItemStateTable it_803F5FF0[]` to `src/melee/it/items/itharisen.c` with 10 state entries covering Harisen unknown motion animation, physics, and collision callbacks.

The new state table entries use `itHarisen_UnkMotion0_*`, `itHarisen_UnkMotion1_*`, `itHarisen_UnkMotion6_*`, `itHarisen_UnkMotion7_Coll`, `itHarisen_UnkMotion8_*`, and `itHarisen_UnkMotion9_*`, with several collision callbacks intentionally set to `NULL`.
Changed `itHarisen_Logic24_Dropped` to call `Item_8026849C(gobj)` instead of `it_8026B390(gobj)` before `Item_80268E5C(gobj, 8, 6)`.

Updated `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` so multiple `.sdata` and `.sdata2` constants formerly named as global `it_804D...` symbols are now local labels such as `@254`, `@255`, `@168` through `@476`, matching local constant usage rather than exporting them globally.
smart_moves: Finalization included both source-level changes and build metadata: the object was only marked matching after `itharisen.c` had the missing state table and call-site correction.
The PR corrected symbol scope in `symbols.txt` from global item-prefixed names to local `@NNN` labels, which is a useful cleanup when constants are not real externally referenced symbols.
The state table was reconstructed explicitly instead of leaving state metadata implicit or generated elsewhere, preserving item behavior layout needed for matching.

The call replacement in dropped logic suggests careful comparison against the target binary: two item helper calls may appear similar, but using the right helper was necessary before declaring the object matching.
decomp_lessons: When finalizing an item file, verify the item state table is present with exact callback ordering, state IDs, and `NULL` collision slots; small table differences can block matching.
Do not leave local float, double, or string constants as global `it_804D...` symbols if the binary/compiler output treats them as local labels; symbol scope affects clean matching and symbol hygiene.
Marking an object as `Matching` in `configure.py` should be paired with source and symbol-table updates that explain why the object now matches.
Similar item helper functions are not interchangeable for matching. In this PR, `Item_8026849C` was required where `it_8026B390` had previously been used.

For item dropped logic, preserve the sequence of setting animation frame speed, resetting `ip` from `gobj->user_data`, applying item scale from `xCC_item_attr->x60_scale`, invoking the item helper, and then calling `Item_80268E5C`.
naming_conventions: Harisen item callbacks use the `itHarisen_` prefix followed by motion labels such as `UnkMotion0`, `UnkMotion6`, `UnkMotion8`, and callback role suffixes `_Anim`, `_Phys`, `_Coll`.
The item state table retained an address-based generated name, `it_803F5FF0`, rather than receiving a semantic name in this PR.
Local constants in `symbols.txt` use `@NNN` labels with `scope:local`; global item symbols use `it_804D...` names with `scope:global`.
Known item library/helper functions use both `it_802...` generated names and `Item_802...` names; this PR specifically preferred `Item_8026849C` at the corrected call site.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduce exact static data, here `ItemStateTable it_803F5FF0[]`, to match the binary data layout for an item object.

Adjust symbol visibility in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` so constants compile/link as local labels where appropriate, including `.sdata` strings and `.sdata2` floats/doubles with correct alignment and size metadata.
Swap a near-equivalent helper call to the exact target helper (`Item_8026849C`) to recover matching codegen in `itHarisen_Logic24_Dropped`.
After the source and symbol changes, move the object from `NonMatching` to `Matching` in the build configuration.
follow_up_queries: Find other item finalization PRs that add `ItemStateTable` definitions before marking objects matching.
Search for remaining item files in `configure.py` still marked `NonMatching` near `itflipper.c`, `itsscope.c`, or other item objects.
Review uses of `it_8026B390` versus `Item_8026849C` in item dropped logic to identify other possible helper misidentifications.
Search `symbols.txt` for global `it_804D...` `.sdata2` constants that may actually be local `@NNN` labels.
